feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core can operate the I2C bus at 100 Kbps normal mode.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use only provided requirement information; measure SCL/SDA behavior in 100 Kbps mode.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps operation using the available prescale mechanism.
Enable the core.
Initiate an I2C transfer on SCL/SDA.
Measure the resulting bus bit rate on SCL.
expected_results: The core performs I2C transfers on SCL/SDA.
Measured I2C operation corresponds to 100 Kbps normal mode.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core can operate the I2C bus at 400 Kbps fast mode.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use only provided requirement information; measure SCL/SDA behavior in 400 Kbps mode.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps operation using the available prescale mechanism.
Enable the core.
Initiate an I2C transfer on SCL/SDA.
Measure the resulting bus bit rate on SCL.
expected_results: The core performs I2C transfers on SCL/SDA.
Measured I2C operation corresponds to 400 Kbps fast mode.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes and responds through the specified WISHBONE Classic interface signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Compliance is limited to the signals and behavior explicitly provided in the specification.
test_steps: Connect to wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_dat_o, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o, and wb_inta_o.
Perform representative WISHBONE read and write accesses to valid register addresses.
Observe acknowledgment and data behavior on the interface.
expected_results: The core interfaces through the listed WISHBONE signals.
Read and write accesses are accepted through the WISHBONE Classic interface.
wb_ack_o responds to accesses and wb_inta_o is available as the interrupt output.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two wb_clk_i cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Count access timing only from asserted request handshake inputs to acknowledgment.
test_steps: Apply a WISHBONE write access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i with valid address/data.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
Repeat for a WISHBONE read access.
Compare observed latency for each access.
expected_results: Each write access takes two wb_clk_i cycles.
Each read access takes two wb_clk_i cycles.

feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level must be configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset polarity is configurable using ARST_LVL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Test both ARST_LVL settings using arst_i only.
test_steps: Set ARST_LVL to active high configuration.
Assert arst_i high and observe reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and confirm reset release.
Set ARST_LVL to active low configuration.
Assert arst_i low and observe reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and confirm reset release.
expected_results: With ARST_LVL set active high, arst_i high resets the core.
With ARST_LVL set active low, arst_i low resets the core.
Reset behavior follows the configured polarity.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify all external pad interface signals for SCL and SDA are present and functional.
feature_list.test_constraints: Verification is limited to the listed signals and their interface roles.
test_steps: Inspect the core interface for scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, scl_pad_oe, sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Drive pad inputs and observe that pad outputs/output-enables are produced during bus activity.
Initiate representative transfers and monitor all six signals.
expected_results: The core exposes scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, scl_pad_oe, sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
The signals participate in external bus activity as input, output, and output-enable connections.

feature_list.requirement_text: Devices connected to SCL and SDA must use open-drain or open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the external bus connection behavior is compatible with open-drain/open-collector signaling.
feature_list.test_constraints: Specification states an electrical requirement for connected devices; core-side verification is limited to compatibility through pad/output-enable behavior.
test_steps: Connect the core pads to an open-drain style bus model for SCL and SDA.
Run representative bus transactions.
Observe whether the core behavior is consistent with shared open-drain/open-collector bus usage.
expected_results: The core external bus behavior is compatible with open-drain/open-collector connections on SCL and SDA.

feature_list.requirement_text: The SCL and SDA output-enable signals must control whether the core drives or releases the external bus lines.
feature_list.test_description: Verify scl_pad_oe and sda_pad_oe control drive versus release of the bus lines.
feature_list.test_constraints: Assume external open-drain style connection; verify control relation only from provided signals.
test_steps: Monitor scl_pad_o, scl_pad_oe, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe during idle and active transfers.
Observe bus behavior when output-enable is asserted for SCL and SDA.
Observe bus behavior when output-enable is deasserted for SCL and SDA.
expected_results: When scl_pad_oe is asserted, the core drives/releases SCL according to output behavior.
When scl_pad_oe is deasserted, the core releases SCL.
When sda_pad_oe is asserted, the core drives/releases SDA according to output behavior.
When sda_pad_oe is deasserted, the core releases SDA.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERlo must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale low-byte register at address 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo is an 8-bit RW register at WISHBONE address 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE accesses only.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit value to address 0x00.
Read back from address 0x00.
Repeat with a second 8-bit value to confirm overwrite behavior.
expected_results: Address 0x00 accepts 8-bit writes.
Reads from address 0x00 return the written low-byte prescale value.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERhi must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale high-byte register at address 0x01.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERhi is an 8-bit RW register at WISHBONE address 0x01.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE accesses only.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit value to address 0x01.
Read back from address 0x01.
Repeat with a second 8-bit value to confirm overwrite behavior.
expected_results: Address 0x01 accepts 8-bit writes.
Reads from address 0x01 return the written high-byte prescale value.

feature_list.requirement_text: CTR must be an 8-bit read/write Control register at address 0x02.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR is an 8-bit RW register at WISHBONE address 0x02.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE accesses only.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit value to address 0x02.
Read back from address 0x02.
Repeat with a second 8-bit value to confirm overwrite behavior.
expected_results: Address 0x02 accepts 8-bit writes.
Reads from address 0x02 return the written control value, subject to defined/reserved bit behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: TXR must be an 8-bit write-only Transmit register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR is an 8-bit write-only register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read behavior is not defined for TXR because address 0x03 is read as RXR.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit value to address 0x03 with wb_we_i asserted.
Initiate a write-based transfer that uses the transmit register contents.
Observe transmitted SDA bit pattern.
expected_results: Address 0x03 accepts 8-bit writes for TXR.
The written byte is used as transmit data for bus activity.

feature_list.requirement_text: RXR must be an 8-bit read-only Receive register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR is an 8-bit read-only register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read-only property is checked via WISHBONE access behavior and received data path.
test_steps: Cause the core to receive a byte from the I2C bus.
Read address 0x03.
Attempt a write to address 0x03 and then perform another receive/read sequence.
expected_results: Reads from address 0x03 return the received byte in RXR.
Write access does not provide writable RXR storage behavior.

feature_list.requirement_text: CR must be an 8-bit write-only Command register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR is an 8-bit write-only register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read behavior at address 0x04 maps to SR, not CR.
test_steps: Write command values to address 0x04 with wb_we_i asserted.
Observe resulting command-driven bus/status behavior.
Read address 0x04 and confirm returned data corresponds to status path rather than retained command data.
expected_results: Address 0x04 accepts command writes.
Written command bits affect core operation.
Reads from address 0x04 do not expose a readable CR image.

feature_list.requirement_text: SR must be an 8-bit read-only Status register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR is an 8-bit read-only register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read-only property is checked via status visibility and non-writability.
test_steps: Read address 0x04 in idle state.
Perform a transfer to change status conditions.
Read address 0x04 again.
Attempt to write address 0x04 and verify only command semantics apply, not writable SR storage.
expected_results: Reads from address 0x04 return status information.
SR contents reflect operational status.
SR is not directly writable as a stored register image.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register must configure the SCL clock using the WISHBONE input clock and the 5SCL prescale formula.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo/PRERhi determine SCL frequency from wb_clk_i using the documented 5*SCL minus 1 relationship.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires measurable wb_clk_i and SCL; verify against the provided formula only.
test_steps: Apply a known wb_clk_i frequency.
Program PRERlo/PRERhi to a selected prescale value.
Enable the core and start a transfer.
Measure SCL frequency.
Compare measured SCL frequency against the programmed prescale using the 5*SCL minus 1 formula.
expected_results: SCL frequency is derived from wb_clk_i.
Measured SCL matches the programmed prescale relationship based on the 5*SCL formula.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register value must only be changed when the EN bit is cleared.
feature_list.test_description: Verify prescale changes are only intended when EN is cleared.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule; direct hardware enforcement is not specified.
test_steps: Clear EN and write new values to PRERlo/PRERhi.
Enable the core and perform a transfer using the new setting.
While EN is set, attempt to write different prescale values and observe whether specification-defined enforcement exists.
expected_results: Prescale programming is successfully performed when EN is cleared.
No hardware-only enforcement requirement beyond the software rule is assumed from the specification.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register reset value must be 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRER reset value is 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check reset state immediately after reset.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read address 0x00 for PRERlo.
Read address 0x01 for PRERhi.
expected_results: PRERlo resets to 0xFF.
PRERhi resets to 0xFF.
Combined prescale reset value is 0xFFFF.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 7 must be the read/write EN bit used to enable or disable the I2C core.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 7 is RW EN and controls core enable/disable.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use WISHBONE access to CTR and command response observation.
test_steps: Write CTR with bit 7 cleared.
Attempt to issue a command.
Write CTR with bit 7 set.
Read back CTR and issue a command again.
expected_results: CTR bit 7 is writable and readable as EN.
When EN=0, the core is disabled for new command response.
When EN=1, the core is enabled for new command response.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must only respond to new commands when EN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify new commands are accepted only when EN is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Focus on new command response only.
test_steps: Clear EN in CTR.
Write a command to CR.
Observe that no new command execution occurs.
Set EN in CTR.
Write the same command to CR again.
Observe command execution.
expected_results: With EN cleared, new commands are not acted upon.
With EN set, new commands are acted upon.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software must only clear EN when no transfer is in progress, after a STOP command, or when STO is set.
feature_list.test_description: Capture the specified software rule for clearing EN relative to transfer state.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a software usage rule; hardware enforcement is not explicitly specified.
test_steps: Start a transfer and monitor TIP and STO-related state.
Clear EN after transfer completion or after STOP issuance.
Repeat a scenario attempting to clear EN during active transfer.
expected_results: The documented valid software usage is to clear EN only when no transfer is in progress, after STOP, or when STO is set.
No additional hardware enforcement is inferred beyond the stated rule.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 6 must be the read/write IEN bit used to enable or disable interrupt generation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 6 is RW IEN and controls interrupt output generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use pending interrupt conditions and wb_inta_o observation.
test_steps: Write CTR with IEN=0.
Create an interrupt-pending condition.
Observe wb_inta_o.
Write CTR with IEN=1.
Create the same interrupt-pending condition again.
Observe wb_inta_o and read back CTR.
expected_results: CTR bit 6 is writable and readable as IEN.
With IEN=0, pending interrupt does not assert processor interrupt request.
With IEN=1, pending interrupt asserts wb_inta_o.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Control Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read address 0x02.
expected_results: CTR reads back as 0x00 after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR stores the transmitted byte and TXR bit 0 serves as data LSB or RW bit depending on transfer type.
feature_list.test_constraints: Need one data transfer and one slave address transfer scenario.
test_steps: Write a known data byte to TXR and initiate a data write transfer.
Observe SDA bits for the transmitted byte.
Write a slave address byte to TXR with bit 0 set to 1 and initiate slave address transfer.
Repeat with bit 0 set to 0.
Observe the first transferred byte on SDA in each case.
expected_results: For data transfer, TXR[0] is transmitted as the byte LSB.
For slave address transfer, TXR[0] is transmitted as the RW bit.
TXR holds the programmed byte used for transmission.

feature_list.requirement_text: During a slave address transfer, RW value 1 must indicate reading from the slave and RW value 0 must indicate writing to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address RW encoding uses 1 for read and 0 for write.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies during slave address transfer only.
test_steps: Load TXR with a slave address byte whose bit 0 is 1 and initiate address transfer.
Observe subsequent transfer direction behavior.
Load TXR with a slave address byte whose bit 0 is 0 and initiate address transfer.
Observe subsequent transfer direction behavior.
expected_results: RW=1 corresponds to reading from the slave.
RW=0 corresponds to writing to the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: TXR is write-only; direct readback is not specified.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Without writing TXR, initiate a transmit scenario only if design observability permits default transmit content inference.
expected_results: TXR reset value is specified as 0x00, but direct readback is not available from the provided interface.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain the last byte received via I2C.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR contains the last byte received from the I2C bus.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires external I2C data source.
test_steps: Cause the core to receive a known byte from the I2C bus.
Wait for transfer completion.
Read RXR at address 0x03.
expected_results: RXR contains the last byte received via I2C.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read address 0x03 before any receive operation.
expected_results: RXR reads as 0x00 after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 7 must be the write-only STA bit used to generate a START or Repeated START condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 7 acts as write-only STA command for START/Repeated START generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Readback of command bits is defined separately; observe bus behavior.
test_steps: Enable the core.
Write CR with STA set together with a transfer command.
Observe SDA/SCL for START generation.
Issue another STA during an active transaction without prior STOP.
Observe SDA/SCL for Repeated START generation.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 7 requests START behavior.
A START or Repeated START condition is generated on the bus as commanded.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 6 must be the write-only STO bit used to generate a STOP condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 6 acts as write-only STO command for STOP generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus behavior on SDA/SCL.
test_steps: Begin an I2C transaction.
Write CR with STO set.
Observe SDA and SCL at transaction end.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 6 requests STOP behavior.
A STOP condition is generated on the bus.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 5 must be the write-only RD bit used to initiate a read from the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 5 acts as write-only RD command to initiate slave read.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires slave/read data source.
test_steps: Address a slave for read or enter a read phase.
Write CR with RD set.
Observe that the core performs a read transfer and updates receive/status state.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 5 initiates a read from the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 4 must be the write-only WR bit used to initiate a write to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 4 acts as write-only WR command to initiate slave write.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires transmit byte in TXR.
test_steps: Load TXR with a known byte.
Write CR with WR set.
Observe SDA/SCL for byte transmission to the slave.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 4 initiates a write to the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 3 must be the write-only ACK bit used by a receiver to send ACK when 0 or NACK when 1.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 3 controls ACK/NACK driven by the receiver.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires receive context with ninth clock acknowledgment phase.
test_steps: Perform a read transaction with ACK command bit written as 0.
Observe acknowledgment on the ninth SCL clock.
Perform a read transaction with ACK command bit written as 1.
Observe acknowledgment on the ninth SCL clock.
expected_results: ACK=0 causes an ACK response.
ACK=1 causes a NACK response.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 0 must be the write-only IACK bit used to clear a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 0 clears a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires IF pending condition.
test_steps: Generate a pending interrupt so IF is set.
If IEN is set, observe wb_inta_o assertion.
Write CR with IACK set.
Read status and observe interrupt output.
expected_results: Writing IACK clears the pending interrupt indication.
IF is cleared after interrupt acknowledge.
wb_inta_o deasserts when the pending interrupt is cleared and interrupt output had been asserted.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify command bits auto-clear and read as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read of address 0x04 returns SR; direct CR read image is not available.
test_steps: Write command values asserting STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK in separate operations.
Allow each command to be accepted/executed.
Read address 0x04 and inspect returned value semantics.
Attempt repeated command issue to confirm no persistent command state remains.
expected_results: STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK do not persist as latched readable ones.
Command bits are automatically cleared after command acceptance.
Any read observation does not show these command bits as ones.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Command Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: CR is write-only at address 0x04; direct readback is not specified.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read address 0x04 only to confirm status path behavior if needed.
Check that no command action is pending after reset.
expected_results: No command bits are active after reset.
Direct CR readback of 0x00 is not observable from the provided interface.

feature_list.requirement_text: RxACK must indicate whether acknowledge was received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RxACK reports acknowledge receipt status from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: Need one ACK and one NACK response case.
test_steps: Perform an address or write transfer to a slave that acknowledges.
Read SR and capture RxACK.
Perform an address or write transfer that is not acknowledged.
Read SR and capture RxACK.
expected_results: RxACK=0 when acknowledge is received.
RxACK=1 when no acknowledge is received.

feature_list.requirement_text: BUSY must indicate whether the I2C bus is busy, setting after START detection and clearing after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify BUSY sets after START detection and clears after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe status around START and STOP conditions.
test_steps: Read SR before bus activity and note BUSY.
Generate a START condition.
Read SR during the active transaction.
Generate a STOP condition.
Read SR after STOP.
expected_results: BUSY=0 before START.
BUSY=1 after START is detected.
BUSY=0 after STOP is detected.

feature_list.requirement_text: AL must indicate arbitration lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL sets when arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires arbitration loss scenario.
test_steps: Create a bus contention scenario that causes the core to lose arbitration.
Read SR after the event.
expected_results: AL is set when arbitration is lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when a STOP signal is detected but was not requested.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL is set when an unrequested STOP is detected.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires external bus event injection.
test_steps: Begin a transfer without issuing STO.
Externally force or model a STOP condition on the bus.
Read SR after STOP detection.
expected_results: AL is set when a STOP condition is detected that was not requested by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when the master drives SDA high but SDA is low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL is set when the core attempts SDA high while observed SDA is low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires contention between sda_pad_o intent and sda_pad_i observation.
test_steps: Create a transfer scenario where the master releases/drives SDA high intent.
Force sda_pad_i low during that condition.
Read SR after the contention.
expected_results: AL is set when the master drives SDA high but the observed SDA line is low.

feature_list.requirement_text: TIP must indicate whether a transfer is in progress.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP indicates active versus completed transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe status during and after transfer.
test_steps: Initiate a transfer.
Read SR while byte transfer is active.
Read SR after transfer completion.
expected_results: TIP=1 while transfer is in progress.
TIP=0 when transfer is complete.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must indicate whether an interrupt is pending.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF indicates interrupt pending state.
feature_list.test_constraints: Need a condition that sets IF and a mechanism to clear it.
test_steps: Read SR in idle state.
Generate a transfer-complete or arbitration-lost condition.
Read SR to observe IF.
Clear pending interrupt with IACK and read SR again.
expected_results: IF=0 when no interrupt is pending.
IF=1 when an interrupt is pending.
IF clears after interrupt acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: A pending interrupt must cause a processor interrupt request when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify pending interrupt drives wb_inta_o only when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires IF pending condition.
test_steps: Set IEN=0 and generate a pending interrupt.
Observe wb_inta_o.
Set IEN=1 and generate a pending interrupt.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: With IEN=0, pending interrupt does not assert processor interrupt request.
With IEN=1, pending interrupt asserts wb_inta_o.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must be set when one byte transfer has completed or arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF is set after byte completion and after arbitration loss.
feature_list.test_constraints: Need two separate scenarios.
test_steps: Complete a single byte transfer and read SR.
Create an arbitration lost condition and read SR.
expected_results: IF is set when one byte transfer has completed.
IF is set when arbitration is lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Status Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: Check immediately after reset.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read address 0x04.
expected_results: SR reads as 0x00 after reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved bits read as zero and document write-as-zero expectation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Readback is verifiable; write-as-zero is a usage rule expectation.
test_steps: Write nonzero patterns affecting reserved positions in CTR, CR, or observe SR reserved bits as applicable.
Read back CTR and SR relevant fields.
Observe reserved bit values.
expected_results: Reserved bits read as zero.
The specification expectation is that writable reserved bits should be written as zero for forward compatibility.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data must be transferred between a Master and Slave synchronously to SCL on SDA, byte by byte.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data transfer occurs on SDA synchronously with SCL and in byte units.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe on-bus signaling only.
test_steps: Initiate a write or read transfer.
Monitor SCL and SDA through one or more bytes.
Group observed SDA bits by SCL clocking.
expected_results: Data on SDA is synchronized to SCL.
Transfer occurs byte by byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each I2C data byte must be 8 bits long and transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transmitted/received I2C bytes are 8 bits and MSB-first.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a known byte pattern on the bus.
test_steps: Load or source a known byte with distinguishable MSB/LSB pattern.
Perform a transfer.
Count SCL pulses for the data byte and inspect SDA bit order.
expected_results: Each data byte spans 8 data bits.
The most significant bit is transmitted first.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each transferred byte must be followed by an acknowledge bit on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify every byte transfer is followed by an acknowledge phase on the ninth SCL cycle.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe complete byte transfer with acknowledgment.
test_steps: Perform a byte transfer on the I2C bus.
Count SCL cycles across the byte and following acknowledgment phase.
Observe SDA during the ninth clock.
expected_results: After each 8-bit byte, an acknowledge bit occurs on the ninth SCL clock cycle.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data bits must be sampled during SCL high, and SDA must remain stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA is stable during SCL high and data sampling aligns with SCL high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe timing relationship on the bus.
test_steps: Initiate a data transfer.
Monitor SDA transitions relative to SCL high and low periods.
Check sampling/stability windows for several bits.
expected_results: Data bits are sampled during SCL high.
SDA remains stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
SDA changes only during SCL low.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a standard transaction follows START, address, data, STOP sequence.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe complete transaction flow.
test_steps: Initiate a standard master transaction.
Monitor bus activity from beginning to end.
Identify START, slave address byte, data byte(s), and STOP.
expected_results: The observed communication sequence is START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.

feature_list.requirement_text: A START signal must be a high-to-low transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START condition timing on SDA and SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus waveform at command initiation.
test_steps: Cause the core to generate START.
Monitor SDA and SCL around the event.
expected_results: START is observed as SDA transitioning high-to-low while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: A Repeated START must be a START signal generated without first generating a STOP signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify Repeated START occurs without preceding STOP.
feature_list.test_constraints: Must occur within an active transaction.
test_steps: Start a transaction.
Without issuing STOP, command another START.
Monitor SDA/SCL around the repeated event.
expected_results: A START condition is generated without a preceding STOP condition.
This event constitutes a Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must generate a START signal when STA is set and either RD or WR is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START is generated when STA is combined with RD or WR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Test both STA+RD and STA+WR combinations.
test_steps: Write CR with STA and WR set.
Observe bus for START generation.
Write CR with STA and RD set.
Observe bus for START generation.
expected_results: START is generated when STA and WR are set.
START is generated when STA and RD are set.

feature_list.requirement_text: A STOP signal must be a low-to-high transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STOP condition timing on SDA and SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus waveform at transaction end.
test_steps: Cause the core to generate STOP.
Monitor SDA and SCL around the event.
expected_results: STOP is observed as SDA transitioning low-to-high while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: The first byte transferred by the master after a START signal must be the slave address with a seven-bit address followed by an RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify first byte after START is 7-bit slave address plus RW bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus immediately after START.
test_steps: Initiate a transaction with a known slave address/RW value in TXR.
Monitor the first byte sent after START.
Decode the 8 transmitted bits.
expected_results: The first byte after START is the slave address byte.
Bits [7:1] are the seven-bit address and bit [0] is the RW bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: The addressed slave must acknowledge by pulling SDA low at the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify addressed slave acknowledgment appears as SDA low on the ninth SCL cycle.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires an acknowledging slave model/device.
test_steps: Address a responding slave.
Monitor SDA and SCL during the address acknowledgment phase.
Count to the ninth SCL clock.
expected_results: The addressed slave pulls SDA low on the ninth SCL clock cycle to acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must support 10-bit slave addresses by generating two address transfers.
feature_list.test_description: Verify 10-bit addressing is performed using two address transfers.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires 10-bit addressing scenario.
test_steps: Prepare a 10-bit slave addressing transaction.
Initiate the transaction.
Monitor the address phase on SDA/SCL and count address transfers.
expected_results: The core performs two address transfers for a 10-bit slave address.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must treat a slave address transfer as a write action using the Transmit Register and WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address transfer is initiated by loading TXR and asserting WR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe bus behavior after TXR write and WR command.
test_steps: Write a slave address byte into TXR.
Set WR in CR.
Monitor the bus for transmission of the slave address byte.
expected_results: The core transfers the slave address from TXR when WR is set.
Slave address transfer is handled as a write action.

feature_list.requirement_text: After successful slave addressing, data transfer must proceed byte by byte in the direction specified by the RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify post-address data direction follows RW bit and proceeds byte by byte.
feature_list.test_constraints: Need one read-direction and one write-direction case after successful addressing.
test_steps: Perform a transaction with RW indicating write and successful slave acknowledgment.
Observe subsequent byte-by-byte write data transfer.
Perform a transaction with RW indicating read and successful slave acknowledgment.
Observe subsequent byte-by-byte read data transfer.
expected_results: After successful addressing, data proceeds byte by byte.
Transfer direction matches the RW bit value.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave signals No Acknowledge, the master may generate a STOP or Repeated START signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify NACK condition allows STOP or Repeated START generation.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is a protocol rule describing allowed behavior after NACK.
test_steps: Create a No Acknowledge condition from the slave.
After NACK, issue STO and observe bus behavior.
Repeat and after NACK issue STA for Repeated START.
expected_results: Following NACK, the master can generate STOP.
Following NACK, the master can generate Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: To write data to a slave, software must store the data in the Transmit Register and set the WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the documented software write sequence causes data transmission.
feature_list.test_constraints: Software usage rule verified through resulting behavior.
test_steps: Write a known data byte to TXR.
Set WR in CR.
Monitor the bus for data transmission.
expected_results: The byte written to TXR is transmitted when WR is set.

feature_list.requirement_text: To read data from a slave, software must set the RD bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the documented software read sequence initiates a slave read.
feature_list.test_constraints: Software usage rule verified through resulting behavior.
test_steps: Prepare the bus for a read phase from a slave.
Set RD in CR.
Monitor transfer and read RXR after completion.
expected_results: Setting RD initiates a read from the slave.
Received data becomes available in RXR after completion.

feature_list.requirement_text: When a transfer is done, the core must reset TIP and set IF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion clears TIP and sets IF.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe status before and after completion.
test_steps: Initiate a byte transfer.
Read SR while active.
Read SR immediately after completion.
expected_results: TIP is 1 while transfer is active.
At transfer completion, TIP resets to 0 and IF becomes 1.

feature_list.requirement_text: When interrupt generation is enabled and a transfer is done, the core must generate an interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion generates wb_inta_o when interrupt generation is enabled.
feature_list.test_constraints: IEN must be set.
test_steps: Set IEN=1.
Initiate and complete a transfer.
Observe IF and wb_inta_o.
expected_results: After transfer completion, IF is set.
wb_inta_o is asserted when interrupt generation is enabled.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain valid data after IF has been set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR contains valid received data once IF is asserted.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires read transfer yielding known data.
test_steps: Perform a read transfer from a slave returning known data.
Wait until IF is set.
Read RXR.
expected_results: After IF is set, RXR contains valid received data.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software may issue a new read or write command when TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_description: Verify new RD/WR commands can be issued once TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_constraints: Software usage rule verified through command acceptance after completion.
test_steps: Complete a transfer and confirm TIP=0.
Issue a new WR or RD command.
Observe that the new command is accepted and executed.
expected_results: When TIP is reset, a new read or write command can be issued successfully.

feature_list.requirement_text: When two or more masters try to control the bus simultaneously, clock synchronization must determine the bus clock.
feature_list.test_description: Verify simultaneous master activity results in synchronized bus clock behavior.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires multi-master test environment.
test_steps: Create a scenario with two masters attempting bus control simultaneously.
Monitor the shared SCL bus clock.
Observe resulting synchronized clock behavior.
expected_results: Clock synchronization determines the bus clock under simultaneous multi-master control.

feature_list.requirement_text: During clock synchronization, SCL must be held low by the device with the longest low period and held high by the device with the shortest high period.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL low/high periods follow multi-master synchronization rules.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires two masters with different low/high timing characteristics.
test_steps: Configure two masters with different SCL low and high periods.
Start simultaneous bus control attempts.
Measure resulting shared SCL low and high durations.
expected_results: The shared SCL low period matches the device with the longest low period.
The shared SCL high period matches the device with the shortest high period.

feature_list.requirement_text: Slave devices may slow the transfer bit rate by holding SCL low after the master has driven SCL low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave clock stretching by holding SCL low after master drives it low is tolerated.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires slave capable of clock stretching.
test_steps: Initiate a transfer to a slave model/device configured to hold SCL low after the master drives it low.
Monitor SCL behavior and transaction progress.
expected_results: The slave can hold SCL low after the master drives it low.
The transfer bit rate is correspondingly slowed.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave SCL low period is greater than the master SCL low period, the resulting SCL bus low period must be stretched and wait states inserted.
feature_list.test_description: Verify longer slave SCL low period stretches the bus low period and inserts wait states.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires controllable slave clock stretching duration.
test_steps: Configure a slave to hold SCL low longer than the master's nominal low period.
Initiate a transfer.
Measure resulting bus SCL low duration and observe transfer pacing.
expected_results: The SCL bus low period is stretched to the longer slave-driven low time.
Wait states are inserted into the transfer.
